description: Execution control rules. 22 rules covering subprocess control, container isolation, privilege escalation prevention, and resource limits.

rules.description: NEVER allow subprocess execution directly on the host OS
rules.rationale: Direct host execution bypasses all sandbox isolation; ALWAYS use sandbox_executor

rules.description: NEVER allow network access from sandbox unless explicitly declared in capability manifest
rules.rationale: Undeclared network access is a sandbox escape and data exfiltration vector

rules.description: NEVER allow writable host filesystem mount except designated /tmp and /workspace
rules.rationale: Writable host mounts enable permanent system modification from sandbox

rules.description: ALWAYS use a fresh sandbox for each execution — never reuse containers
rules.rationale: Container reuse creates persistent state that can be exploited across executions

rules.description: ALWAYS verify container image integrity via SHA-256 before execution
rules.requirement: image_sha256_verified == true or env == 'development' with signed waiver
rules.rationale: Prevents execution of tampered or backdoored container images

rules.description: NEVER allow privileged container mode or --privileged flag
rules.rationale: Privileged mode grants full host access, completely negating sandbox isolation

rules.description: NEVER allow setuid/setgid binary execution in sandbox
rules.rationale: setuid/setgid binaries enable privilege escalation within and beyond sandbox

rules.description: NEVER allow ptrace system call or process tracing in sandbox
rules.rationale: ptrace enables process injection and credential theft from other processes

rules.description: NEVER allow mount system calls inside sandbox
rules.rationale: Mounting enables filesystem manipulation that can escape the container

rules.description: NEVER allow kernel module loading or unloading
rules.rationale: Kernel modules have unrestricted kernel-level access; loading = total compromise

rules.description: NEVER allow chroot or pivot_root in sandbox
rules.rationale: chroot/pivot_root can be used to break out of container filesystem isolation

rules.description: NEVER allow CAP_SYS_ADMIN or CAP_NET_ADMIN capabilities
rules.rationale: These capabilities grant administrator-equivalent privileges in containers

rules.description: ALWAYS enforce resource limits per declared tiered profile (light/standard/heavy). Light: 1 CPU / 512 Mi / 60s. Standard: 2 CPU / 4 Gi / 600s. Heavy: 4 CPU / 8 Gi / 600s. No execution may exceed the heavy profile maximum without human approval.
rules.requirement: resources.profile in ['light', 'standard', 'heavy'] and resources.cpus <= 4 and resources.memory <= 8589934592 and resources.timeout <= 600
rules.rationale: Tiered profiles prevent DoS via resource exhaustion while allowing workload-appropriate sizing. Raw resource declarations are normalized to the nearest profile and capped at heavy maximum. Extended resources require human approval.

rules.description: NEVER allow fork bombs or unbounded process creation
rules.rationale: Fork bombs exhaust PID space, causing denial of service

rules.description: ALWAYS apply seccomp default profile blocking dangerous syscalls
rules.rationale: Seccomp is the primary syscall filter preventing kernel exploitation

rules.description: NEVER allow user namespace creation (unshare --user)
rules.rationale: User namespaces can be exploited for privilege escalation (CVE-2013-1858, etc.)

rules.description: NEVER allow /proc or /sys write operations
rules.rationale: Writing to proc/sys modifies kernel state and can enable container escape

rules.description: ALWAYS drop all Linux capabilities not explicitly required
rules.rationale: Principle of least privilege: only grant capabilities explicitly needed

rules.description: NEVER allow direct access to Docker socket from sandbox
rules.rationale: Docker socket access = root on host; primary container escape vector

rules.description: NEVER allow execution from /tmp or /dev/shm directories
rules.rationale: Execution from tmp directories is a common malware persistence technique

rules.description: NEVER allow LD_PRELOAD or LD_LIBRARY_PATH manipulation
rules.rationale: Library injection hijacks program execution for privilege escalation

rules.description: ALWAYS enable AppArmor or SELinux profile for sandbox containers
rules.rationale: MAC (Mandatory Access Control) provides defense in depth beyond seccomp
